rows.notes: Consensus between all extractors for row type, description, and empty amounts. Both extractors provided clear and confident readings.

rows.description: Recepta Varia.
rows.notes: Consensus on row type, description, and empty amounts. While gpt-5-mini noted a 'stylised spelling', both ultimately agreed on the transcription. Gemini-flash had higher confidence, and visual inspection confirms the spelling.

rows.description: Of Mr Barnes Balance of Acct of Rent &c. for Wild Parish to Xmas 1831
rows.notes: Amounts are consensus. The description is combined from gemini-flash's two lines (rows 3 & 4), which accurately captures the multi-line braced entry. 'Acct' is visually more accurate than 'Acc.' from gemini-flash. Gemini-flash correctly read 'Xmas', whereas gpt-5-mini misread it as 'Attorns'.
rows.english_description: Balance of rent and related accounts received from Mr Barnes for Wild parish up to Christmas 1831.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Balance of rent and related accounts received from Mr Barnes for Wild parish up to Christmas 1831, amounting to £52 11s 3d.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Reimbursement received from donors for gas lighting in the Rector's lodgings for the half-year to Michaelmas 1832, amounting to £3 5s 7½d.

rows.description: Of Mr Arbuthnot, Bursar, Incrementum Dominus Anrias 1832
rows.notes: Amounts are consensus. Gemini-flash's description is visually much more accurate, especially for the name 'Arbuthnot' (vs 'Able') and the Latin phrase 'Incrementum Dominus Anrias' (vs 'Incrementation Donus'). Gpt-5-mini's note about the description's unclarity supports choosing gemini-flash's more precise reading.
rows.english_description: Increment (incrementum) received from Mr Arbuthnot, the Bursar, for the year 1832.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Increment received from Mr Arbuthnot, the Bursar, for 1832, amounting to £333 9s 9d.
